title: peristylus alpinus
intro: Peristylus alpinus, also known as the alpine orchid or lady's slipper orchid, is a type of terrestrial orchid found in alpine regions of Asia, characterized by its unique, slipper-shaped flowers. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to have medicinal benefits in treating various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Peristylus alpinus include its ability to reduce inflammation, boost the immune system, and exhibit antiviral activity. Herbal preparations of Peristylus alpinus are typically made by drying the roots and leaves, which can be used in infusions, decoctions, or as a powder for medicinal purposes.
